title: acmella alba var. ecuadorensis
intro: Acmella alba var. ecuadorensis, also known as the 'Parasol Mallow' or 'Brazilian Toothache Plant', is a variety of the Acmella alba plant native to Ecuador. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for relieving toothache pain, reducing swelling, and soothing sore throats. The health benefits of Acmella alba var. ecuadorensis include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, making it a popular natural remedy for oral health issues. Acmella alba var. ecuadorensis can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as applied topically as a poultice or oil to the affected area.
